items.original: Меня опять терзают смутные сомнения… У Шпака — магнитофон, у посла — медальон
items.translation:
Vague doubts haunt me once again...
Shpak's tape recorder, the ambassador's medallion.
items.reference: Vague doubts haunt me once again... Shpak's tape recorder, the ambassador's medallion.
items.analysis: Perfect match to the reference. It preserves the mock-heroic register of «терзают смутные сомнения» and keeps the clipped parallelism of «У Шпака — …, у посла — …» without incorrectly turning it into possession. It also correctly implies the comedic linkage between two losses (tape recorder/medallion) rather than simple ownership.

items.original: Здравствуйте, царь, очень приятно. Царь, очень приятно, царь. Очень приятно, здравствуйте, царь.
items.translation: Hello, I’m the Tsar, very pleased to meet you. The Tsar, very pleased. Tsar, very pleased. Hello, the Tsar.
items.reference: Hello, I’m the Tsar, very pleased to meet you. The Tsar, very pleased. Tsar, very pleased. Hello, the Tsar.
items.analysis: Identical to the reference. The translation fully preserves the comic effect: repetitive Soviet-style polite formulae and the absurd self-introduction as “the Tsar,” matching Bunsha’s drunken, mundane etiquette in a royal role.

items.translation: I demand that the banquet continue!
items.reference: I demand that the banquet continue!
items.analysis: Identical to the reference. Keeps the authoritarian, performative tone and the comedic escalation of Bunsha’s power fantasy.

items.original: Оставь меня, старушка, я в печали
items.translation: Leave me, old woman, I am in sorrow.
items.reference: Leave me, old woman, I am in sorrow.
items.analysis: Identical to the reference. Preserves the archaic/high-register melancholy and the comic mismatch with the modern domestic situation; “old woman” keeps the intended insult/condescension.

items.translation: They've walled me in... Walled me in, the demons!
items.reference: They've walled me in... Walled me in, the demons!
items.analysis: Identical to the reference. Correctly treats “demons” as the agent and keeps the repetitive panic rhythm that sells the gag (elevator/technology misread as sorcery).

items.translation: Behold the power of the life-giving cross!
items.reference: Behold the power of the life-giving cross!
items.analysis: Identical to the reference. Maintains the elevated, quasi-biblical proclamation and the joke’s ideological edge (religious certainty in an atheist-modern setting).

items.translation: The floor is open to everyone!
items.reference: The floor is open to everyone!
items.analysis: Identical to the reference. Captures the MC/announcer vibe and the mass-participation command, preserving the comic ‘party decree’ effect rather than a flat literal “Everyone dance.”

items.translation: Now, this was a good call!
items.reference: Now, this was a good call!
items.analysis: Identical to the reference. Keeps the breezy self-satisfaction and the idiomatic feel (a lucky stop-in) that makes Miloslavsky charmingly amoral.

items.original: Икра чёрная, красная… Да! Заморская икра… баклажанная!
items.translation: Black caviar, red caviar... and yes! Overseas spread... eggplant!
items.reference: Black caviar, red caviar... and yes! Overseas spread... eggplant!
items.analysis: Identical to the reference. Crucially avoids calling the eggplant item “caviar” in English, preserving the joke’s inversion without confusing viewers unfamiliar with “eggplant caviar” as a Soviet product category.

items.original: Три магнитофона, три кинокамеры, три портсигара отечественных, куртка… замшевая… Три
items.translation: Three tape recorders, three movie cameras, three cigarette cases, domestic... a suede jacket... Three.
items.reference: Three tape recorders, three movie cameras, three cigarette cases, domestic... a suede jacket... Three.
items.analysis: Identical to the reference. Preserves the comic inventory cadence, the self-important “domestic” qualifier, and the trailing “Three” that signals Shpak’s escalating/exaggerated accounting.

items.original: И тебя вылечат… и тебя тоже вылечат… и меня вылечат…
items.translation: And they’ll cure you... and they’ll cure you too... and they’ll cure me...
items.reference: And they’ll cure you... and they’ll cure you too... and they’ll cure me...
items.analysis: Identical to the reference. Keeps the calm, rhythmic, resigned repetition that turns a frightening institutional reality (psychiatric commitment) into a shared absurdist punchline.

summary.interpretation: 0.00 MQM penalty points per meaning unit (≈ 0.00 major errors per unit; weights: minor=1, major=5, critical=10)
